methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $82M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Firestorm Labs is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 publicly traded companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage vicinity, including NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic. The median valuation across this peer set stands at $1.0B, with a range of $125M to $3.0B.

Two valuation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $82M total-raised proxy, implying a valuation of $164M. Peer-median analysis, based on the 8 same-sector private comparables, yields $1.0B as the implied value. The two methods produce a wide spread reflecting both early-stage funding-based floors and mature-stage peer benchmarks.

Triangulation across these methods supports a valuation range of $139M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $582M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects the heterogeneity within the defense-tech cohort—peer valuations span from $125M to $3.0B—and the inherent uncertainty in early-stage comparability. Investors should weight the peer-median approach cautiously given the Series B-skew of the comp set and product-market maturity differences across the peer universe.
